_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave D2 (population scaling, W16: EM-170 turn-latency guard, EM-158 cadence tiers, EM-159 salience gating, EM-160 spontaneity floor, EM-166 observability, EM-161 prompt diet, EM-162 cache-key normalization, EM-163 tier-gated tools, EM-165 25-agent casting, EM-164 measured verification)

resolution.summary: QE gate for Wave D2 on build/wave-d1-ew-city (B1 361697a, B2 db36ddc, B3 5a23019/90394d2 + B4's EM-165/EM-164). EM-165 ships per the orchestrator decision as a documented VARIANT config — config/world.city25.yaml (NOT the default; world.yaml keeps the 3-agent cast) — 25 distinct persona-library-derived agents: 5 protagonists (Ada/Bram/Cleo + run-248 spawn archetypes Vesper/Marrow, five distinct lanes, all waking in the plaza), 8 supporting (the remaining 8 persona cards incl. run-248 spawns Hazel/Brick/Tilly), 12 background (city-flavored originals seeded at workplaces across all five districts), spread over all 7 real FreeLLMAPI lanes with the high-cap fast lanes (cerebras-glm 6 seats, gemini-flash 5, groq-llama 5) carrying 10/12 background seats; the rpd-50 qwen-next lane carries exactly one seat. The file's world: block is byte-equal to world.yaml except agent_count (pinned by test). EM-164 measured (harness under /tmp/em164/, results p1/p2/p3_results.json): (1) MockProvider engine correctness at 25 — due-set math EXACT over 60 rounds (P 60 / S 20 / B 6 turns per seat; protagonists precisely 10x background), snapshot round-trip at the round-30 boundary byte-identical modulo the documented from_snapshot limits with cadence_tier/reflex_streak/round counters surviving and the restored world holding the math for 10 more rounds, zero crashes in both a 60-round quiet run and a 40-round chaos run with mid-run deaths, invariants green; (2) token/cadence math measured with a counting provider over the real 7-lane Router — 8.38 LLM-consulting turns/round as-shipped and 8.3 exactly in the P1 quiet run vs the v4 table's ~8.3 claim (VALIDATED), prompt diet ordering holds (B 2113 < S 3233 < P 4045 mean chars; max background prompt 2871 < 5000 guard), BUT the realized EM-162 decision-cache hit rate is 0% in every scenario including day-pinned idealized quiet — the v4-assumed 50-60% does NOT materialize at 25 agents (day drift + tick-stamped memory lines + reflex relocation churn; mechanism unit-sound, integration payoff absent — the wave's MAJOR finding); (3) bounded LIVE FreeLLMAPI run (separate headless process, user's :8000 run untouched and verified, 60 calls in 543s) — EM-170 held perfectly under the worst plausible conditions (proxy rerouting to slow reasoning models + contention with the user's own live session): 32/60 calls hit the 12s budget and every one cancelled cleanly at worst 12.01s (= budget + 10ms), zero stalls past the wall, ~84k tokens consumed, projected pace 299 turns/h (44.8 rounds/h) at the 3s tick. Full gates: backend 445/445 (440 baseline + 5 new city25 pins), web 501/501, tsc + vite build clean. Zero blockers; gate GREEN with the cache finding and five honest MINORs handed to plan-intake.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-d2.md §B4 + the orchestrator's gate-time decision by building the roster, reading B1-B3's landed code, and measuring — not by trusting prior reports. EM-165: variant-config decision honored (config/world.city25.yaml documents WHAT/HOW-TO-RUN in its header; world.yaml untouched — diff shows agent_count as the only world-block delta, and test_city25_roster.py pins dataclass equality of the parsed params); 5/8/12 tier split, persona-library derivation (all 10 cards cast; 12 city-flavored originals where the library runs short), all 7 real lanes, fast-lane background weighting (10/12 seats on cerebras/gemini/groq), population cap and agent_count semantics preserved (loader-parsed, 25 == 25). EM-164: every contracted measurement produced with real numbers — MockProvider-first engine correctness at 25 (free-scale law: the quiet P1 run consults the router on exactly 498/532 turns, all 34 background reflex turns prove zero-call resolution via the call-count idiom), bounded live run within its <=120-call budget (60 calls), tokens/turn (~1.4k), realized cache-hit rate reported against the v4 50-60% assumption (it is 0%), turns/hour (299 projected), per-lane pressure vs rpd caps, worst single-turn stall 12.01s <= budget + epsilon with EM-170 active. DOCKED ONE POINT: the EM-162 batch's contracted payoff ('the router's sha1 decision cache can hit on quiet rounds') is falsified at the population this wave targets — B3's unit tests prove the normalization on crafted messages (turns_per_day=999), but at city25's shipped pacing a 25-turn round spans >1 in-world day, and tick-stamped memory lines (an agent's OWN previous action) plus reflex move-home churn make background prompts never repeat: hits are structurally ~0. The free-scale outcome the wave promised still lands (reflex turns REMOVE the calls; 8.3 turn-equivalents/round measured), but the cache discount in the v4 capacity table must be treated as 0 until the normalization is extended.

test_coverage.notes: B4 adds 5 durable pin tests (backend/tests/test_city25_roster.py): 25 distinct tiered agents through the REAL loader, lane totality + fast-lane background weighting + the one-seat qwen rule, location validity, the named-cast protagonist five on five distinct lanes in the plaza, and world-block/places/animals byte-equality with world.yaml modulo agent_count (the variant can never silently fork the physics). The EM-164 measurement harness (throwaway by design, /tmp/em164/: em164_harness.py + p1/p2/p3) exercised what the suite cannot: 60-round 25-agent scheduler math, mid-run snapshot restore + continued scheduling, a 40-round chaos run with a 5-death cascade, counting-provider call/char accounting across the real Router cache, and a live 7-lane run under genuine proxy degradation. Honest gaps, none load-bearing: (1) no in-repo automated test drives the actual 25-agent world end-to-end (the pins are config-shape only; the harness is not committed); (2) the 0% realized cache-hit finding has no failing/xfail test pinning it — a regression in the OTHER direction (a fix) would go unmeasured; (3) the bounded live run ended at round 9 of 15 (the 12s-budget timeouts consumed the 540s wall), so zero LIVE background-tier turns were observed — background cadence + reflex behavior is MockProvider/counting-provider-verified only; (4) the pre-existing mid-round death scheduler skip (see issues) has no regression test.

security.notes: B4's diff is one YAML config variant + one test file — no new endpoints, no new request shapes, no new code paths, zero new LLM surface. The 25 persona texts are repo-authored config (same trust class as the existing world.yaml personalities), parsed by the existing loader with its existing normalization (_norm_cadence_tier falls back to protagonist on anything unknown; profiles validated against the registry at spawn/reassign). No secrets in the variant (profiles.yaml's api_key_env indirection unchanged). The live measurement used the standing FreeLLMAPI key from .env via the existing adapter path; no paid keys touched (standing law upheld — MockProvider for engine verification, FreeLLMAPI only for the bounded live run). The user's running backend and run.sqlite were never written (measurement ran EM_DB_PATH=:memory: in a separate process; /api/state polled read-only).

regression_risk.notes: Lowest-risk batch of the wave: config/world.city25.yaml is NOT loaded by anything until the user deliberately stages it (the loader only reads world.yaml from one config dir), so default behavior is byte-identical; test_city25_roster.py is additive. The full suites prove it: backend 445/445 with the 440 baseline intact, web 501/501, tsc + build clean. The byte-equality pin makes future drift of the variant's physics a test failure instead of a silent fork. Watch items are all OBSERVATIONS about existing behavior at 25 agents, not regressions from this batch: the live timeout rate (53% of calls budget-clamped on a degraded proxy night) means a city25 live run will lean heavily on idle fallbacks until lane health steers routing; qwen-next's 50 rpd cap supports only ~1 protagonist-hour/day at projected pace; and the recharge-to-full energy-band flapping inflates background salience in steady state (P1: 30 of 38 background LLM turns were 'salient', most from band crossings).

frontend.changed_existing: B4 touches no frontend file; suite re-run as the wave gate

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx tsc --noEmit — clean; npm run build — clean in 5.05s (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only)
test_results.qe_probes: EM-164 measurement harness (/tmp/em164/: em164_harness.py, p1_mock_correctness.py, p2_token_math.py, p3_live.py; results in p1/p2/p3_results.json). P1 MockProvider at 25 agents: [quiet 60-round run] due-set math EXACT for all 25 seats (P 60 / S 20 / B 6 per seat = protagonists 10.0x background), snapshot at the round-30 boundary round-trips byte-identically modulo documented from_snapshot limits (beliefs restore empty, running=False), cadence_tier + reflex_streak + round/turn_order/turn_index survive, restored world holds the math for rounds 31-40 (50/24/12), zero crashes, zero malformed-output failures, credits/energy invariants green, every background LLM turn carries a cadence_reason (30 salient + 8 wildcard), reflex resolves 81% of non-salient due turns (34/42; whole-tier share 47% — band-crossing salience, see issues), wildcard rate 0.19 vs configured 0.15 (seeded, small n); [chaos 40-round run, default mock scripts] 5-death starvation cascade (vote-injection artifact) absorbed with zero crashes, dead agents take no turns, 230 world-gate rejections all graceful, and the pre-existing mid-round death scheduler skip reproduced (rounds 29/30/31 each ran one fewer turn than due — see issues). P2 counting-provider over the real 7-lane Router: as-shipped scenario 8.38 LLM-consulting turns/round and quiet scenario 8.12 vs the v4 claim of ~8.3 (VALIDATED — and P1's quiet run landed on 8.30 exactly); prompt diet ordering B 2113 < S 3233 < P 4045 mean chars with max background prompt 2871 < the 5000-char EM-161 guard (~528 mean est. background prompt tokens); realized cache-hit rate 0.0% in as-shipped, idealized-quiet (decay 0, pure idle), AND day-pinned (turns_per_day 100k) scenarios — prompt diffs identify tick-stamped memory lines and reflex-relocation roster/menu churn as the residual key-breakers beyond EM-162's energy-bucket + day-floor; cache bookkeeping itself sound (misses == adapter calls in all three). P3 bounded live FreeLLMAPI (proxy 200-verified, separate in-process engine, EM_DB_PATH=:memory:, user's :8000 session checked and untouched — it went RUNNING mid-measurement, sharing proxy capacity): 60 adapter calls / 60 turns / 9 rounds in 542.8s, EM-170 budget 12s active — 32 llm_timeout turns ALL cancelled at 12.003-12.010s, worst single-turn stall 12.01s <= budget + epsilon (run 248's 14-32s class eliminated), proxy heavily rerouting to reasoning models (nemotron-3-super/command-a-reasoning/mistral-medium; mistral-small the only consistently fast lane at p50 1.4s), per-lane calls vs rpd: gemini 11/1500, qwen 9/50, deepseek 11/-, groq 10/1000, cerebras 11/14400, mistral 4/-, kimi 4/-; tokens ~84.2k total (35.4k provider-reported input + 9.3k output + 39.5k chars/4-estimated input on cancelled/unreported calls); pace 398 raw turns/h, 299 projected at the 3s tick interval = 44.8 rounds/h at 25 agents.

issues.title: Realized EM-162 decision-cache hit rate at 25 agents is 0% — the v4 table assumed 50-60%
issues.detail: Measured via router.cache_stats() over the real 7-lane Router with counting adapters (cacheable traffic, mock bypass not involved): 0 hits / 335 misses as-shipped, 0/325 in an idealized quiet world (decay 0, all-idle), and 0/325 even with the in-world day pinned (turns_per_day 100k). Three compounding causes, confirmed by prompt diffs: (a) DAY DRIFT — at turns_per_day 20 a single 25-agent round consumes >1 in-world day, so a background agent's consecutive due turns (~88 ticks apart) NEVER share the EM-162 day-floored 'Tick: day N' line; (b) TICK-STAMPED MEMORY — every event an agent witnesses, including its OWN previous action ('[tick 167] Pearl idles.'), lands in the prompt with a raw tick number, making each prompt globally unique; (c) REFLEX RELOCATION CHURN — background reflex move-home turns change co-located rosters and the tool-menu target lists (whisper/give/steal/...) for everyone at both places. B3's unit tests remain correct (they prove the normalization on crafted messages with turns_per_day=999); what's absent is the integration payoff at exactly the population the wave targets. Until extended (candidates: drop the day line from background prompts entirely, bucket/drop tick stamps in background memory lines, scope menu target lists the way EM-161 already scopes places), v4 capacity estimates must price every LLM-consulting turn as a real upstream call — which the measured 8.3-8.4 turn-equivalents/round shows is still comfortably within free-lane budgets.

issues.title: Live free lanes spent the measurement night budget-clamped: 53% of calls hit the 12s EM-170 wall
issues.detail: In the bounded live run, 32 of 60 calls were cancelled by the turn budget (p50 call latency = 12.003s, i.e. the budget itself), across every lane except mistral-small (p50 1.4s, 4/4 ok). The proxy was rerouting hard to slow reasoning models (kilo/nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free, cohere/command-a-reasoning, mistral-medium) — almost no calls landed on the natively-requested models — and the user's own live session started mid-measurement, sharing proxy capacity. EM-170 itself behaved flawlessly (every timeout cancelled at 12.003-12.010s; the world never stalled; lane-health demerits recorded), so this is NOT a defect in the wave — it is measured evidence that on a degraded proxy night a 25-agent city would resolve half its protagonist turns as llm_timeout idle fallbacks, which reads as a sleepy city, not a frozen one. Worth a W17-planning look: prefer-fast-lane routing when lane_health shows chronic timeouts, a cheaper default lane mix for protagonists, or a persona-aware retry-on-next-turn. Caveat recorded honestly: contention with the user's run means the 53% figure is a worst-plausible-night bound, not a typical-night estimate.

issues.title: Pre-existing scheduler quirk: an agent dying mid-round skips the next due agent for that round
issues.detail: world.next_agent() -> _rebuild_turn_order() prunes dead ids from the CURRENT round's _turn_order without adjusting _turn_index, so when an agent at a position before the pointer dies mid-round, every later id shifts left by one and the agent at the pointer is silently skipped for the rest of the round. Reproduced deterministically in the P1 chaos run (rounds 29/30/31 each executed one fewer turn than due; e.g. background agent Dottie was in round 30's 24-id due set but never acted — round 30 ran 23 turns). Present since the original engine commit (58a8e7e, pre-petridish emergence/engine/world.py has the identical prune), NOT a Wave D2 regression — the D2 tier scheduler inherited it verbatim. Self-heals at the next round start (full sorted-id rebuild). Cheap fix when convenient: count how many pruned ids sat below _turn_index and decrement it by that amount; a regression test fits naturally in test_wave_d2_cadence.py.

issues.title: Recharge-to-full flaps the EM-159 energy band, making routinely-recharging background agents near-permanently salient
issues.detail: _energy_band puts 100 in its own band (int(e // 25) = 4) while 96 is band 3, so a background agent that recharges to full and then decays 4/turn crosses a band boundary on essentially every due turn — the energy_band salience trigger fires and the agent takes a full LLM turn. In the P1 quiet run this drove most of the 30 'salient' background turns (whole-tier reflex share 47% vs 81% over genuinely non-salient turns), and it is why steady-state background LLM consumption sits at the top of the v4 envelope. Arguably defensible (recharge IS an event), but the band edges at exactly 100/75/50/25 interact badly with the recharge-to-cap loop. Cheap options: hysteresis (only count crossings of a band the agent has LEFT for a full turn), or clamp the top band to [75, 100]. Measured, not contract-violating — EM-159's trigger list says 'energy crossing a threshold band' and it does.

issues.title: qwen-next (50 rpd) cannot sustain a protagonist seat for more than ~1 hour/day at city25 pace
issues.detail: The casting keeps the scarcest lane at exactly one seat (Bram, protagonist — his lane in the existing world.yaml cast, preserved). At the measured projected pace (44.8 rounds/h) a protagonist consumes ~45 calls/h, so the OpenRouter :free 50-requests/day cap is exhausted within the first hour of a continuous city25 run; after that the lane errors into idle fallbacks until the UTC reset (the EM-083 usage_alert fires at 70%). Options when the variant goes live: move Bram to a high-cap lane and hand qwen-next to a supporting/background seat (~15/h or ~4.5/h), or accept the daily budget as Bram's 'screen time'. Left as-shipped because the contract says protagonists keep the existing named cast's lanes, and one capped lane degrades exactly one agent.

issues.title: The bounded live run never reached a background-due round — live reflex behavior is unverified
issues.detail: The 540s wall + 12s-budget-clamped calls ended the live measurement at round 9 of 15; background tier first comes due at round 10, so the live run exercised only protagonist + supporting turns (43 + 17). Background cadence, salience gating, and reflex turns are thoroughly verified on MockProvider and the counting provider (P1/P2), and nothing in the live path branches on adapter type, so risk is low — but a future unbounded live session should confirm reflex turns surface correctly in the real feed (EM-166 chips/markers) at 25 agents. Recorded as the bounded-run's honest coverage edge rather than silently claiming live verification.

issues.title: MockProvider default scripts mass-starve a 25-agent world (mock-only cosmetic)
issues.detail: The default per-name scripts + dynamic vote injection were designed for the 3-5 seat village: at 25 agents, the first propose_rule arms the injection for ALL agents, every agent then attempts townhall-gated votes from wherever it stands (297 graceful world-gate rejections in 60 rounds), the rule can never collect a 13-vote majority because almost nobody is at the town hall, and agents starve from ~round 29 having never recharged (P1 chaos run: Bram/Cleo/Marrow/Vesper/+1 died; useful as a death-path probe, useless as a demo). Affects only `python -m petridish.run --profile mock` with the city25 variant and any future mock-driven 25-agent test. Cheap fix if mock demos at 25 matter: make the injected vote conditional on being at a governance place (mirroring the gate), or teach the generic fallback script to recharge before working.

gate_decision.reason: All Wave D2 hard gates are green and every B4 contract obligation is met with measured numbers: backend 445/445 ('445 passed, 1 warning in 25.45s', 440 baseline intact + 5 new EM-165 pins), web 501/501 across 41 files, tsc and vite build clean. The wave's load-bearing claims survive hostile measurement: the EM-158 due-set math is EXACT at 25 agents over 60 MockProvider rounds and across a mid-run snapshot/restore (protagonists precisely 10x background turn share); the free-scale call math lands on the v4 table's number almost to the decimal (8.30-8.38 measured LLM turn-equivalents/round vs ~8.3 claimed); the EM-161 diet holds live-shaped (background max 2871 < 5000-char guard, B < S < P ordering); and EM-170 passed the strongest possible live test — on a degraded proxy night with the user's own session contending, 32 budget-clamped calls ALL cancelled within 10ms of the 12s wall (worst stall 12.01s; run 248's 14-32s world-freeze class is dead), with lane-health demerits recorded. The one falsified assumption — realized decision-cache hit rate 0% vs the v4-assumed 50-60% — is reported as the wave's MAJOR finding with its mechanism precisely diagnosed (day drift at 25-turn rounds, tick-stamped memory lines, reflex relocation churn); it does not meet the blocker bar because the capacity outcome it was meant to protect is already achieved by the reflex-turn call REMOVAL (8.3 calls/round total is comfortably within every lane budget except qwen-next's 50 rpd, tracked as its own MINOR), and EM-162's unit-level contract artifacts remain true as written. EM-165 ships exactly as the orchestrator decided: a documented variant config the user flips deliberately, with byte-equality of the world physics pinned by test so the variant can never silently fork the default world. Gate rules: zero CRITICAL, contract 4 >= 3, security 5 >= 3. GREEN — W17 may start, with the cache-normalization extension, the live-lane timeout posture, and the five MINORs handed to plan-intake.
